name: Grade 4 Reading — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 4 pathway for complex texts, point of view, figurative language, and text evidence. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 4 knowledge and transferable skills in complex texts, point of view, figurative language, and text evidence.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: complex texts, point of view, figurative language, and text evidence
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about complex texts, point of view, figurative language, and text evidence and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

units.title: Investigate: complex texts, point of view, figurative language, and text evidence
units.essentialQuestion: What question and evidence can guide our study of complex texts, point of view, figurative language, and text evidence?
outcomes: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about complex texts, point of view, figurative language, and text evidence.
I can use appropriate disciplinary vocabulary, evidence, and tools to communicate my reasoning.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about complex texts, point of view, figurative language, and text evidence, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.

successCriteria: The learner completes a grade 4 task connected to complex texts, point of view, figurative language, and text evidence.
The learner uses relevant evidence, vocabulary, or a model to explain reasoning.
The learner revises, seeks feedback, or identifies a specific next step.
